input.headline: Thanksgiving travel tips: Best and worst days to fly or drive

raw.headline: Thanksgiving travel tips: Best and worst days to fly or drive

winner: Why did the turkey refuse to fly on Thanksgiving? It heard the "best days" were actually just a way for airlines to say "we're packed like sardines—you're welcome.

candidates.text: Why did the turkey refuse to fly on Thanksgiving? Because it heard the plane was *fowl* weather for feathers!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'fowl' and 'feathers' are clever and specific to the turkey theme, with good surprise and natural flow."}

candidates.text: Why did I bring a ladder to Thanksgiving? The experts said these are the best *rungs* to travel!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Playful pun on 'rungs' and 'best days,' concise and specific to the travel theme with a surprising twist."}

candidates.text: Turns out the 'best day' to fly for Thanksgiving is also the one where everyone’s so stuffed they nod off mid-takeoff.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on travel tips with a specific, relatable scenario that ties back to Thanksgiving."}

candidates.text: Don't fly on Thanksgiving Day—it's when all the turkeys go on strike!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'turkeys' and 'strike' are clever and specific to the Thanksgiving context, delivering a surprise twist with brevity and natural humor."}

candidates.text: Why did the turkey refuse to fly on Thanksgiving? It heard the "best days" were actually just a way for airlines to say "we're packed like sardines—you're welcome.
